name: Jira
access_notes: Developers can sign up for a free Jira account and generate API tokens via their Atlassian profile to access the REST API. No paid plan or formal approval is required for basic API usage.
mcp_notes: Atlassian has released an official MCP server to allow AI agents and CLI tools to interact with Jira.
api_notes: The Jira Cloud platform REST API is comprehensive, covering core objects such as issues, projects, boards, workflows, and users. The Data Center version also provides a similarly extensive REST API.

evidence.claim: The Jira Cloud platform REST API provides comprehensive access to Jira resources like issues, projects, and workflows.
Developers can authenticate using API tokens (via basic auth) or OAuth 2.0.
Atlassian provides an official MCP server for integrating Jira with CLI tools and AI agents.

one_liner: Jira is a project management and issue-tracking platform that provides a robust REST API for automating workflows, managing issues, and syncing data across external systems.
auth_notes: Basic authentication (using email and an API token) is the standard for individual/server-to-server integrations. OAuth 2.0 (3LO) is used for user-delegated access. JWT is utilized for Atlassian Connect apps. Older methods like OAuth 1.0a are deprecated.
